---
title: "Day 4 — Rebuild micrograd from an empty file"
subtitle: "Monday, 27 July 2026 · Week 1 · Foundations · Study day · 3-4 h"
---

::: {.day-nav .kind-study}
[← Day 3](day-03.qmd) · [All 50 days](../timeline.qmd) · [Day 5 →](day-05.qmd)
:::

::: {.why}
Watching an autograd engine get built and writing one from an empty file are different skills, and only the second one transfers.
:::

## What you should be able to do tonight

- Write `backward()` including the topological sort from memory, and say why the sort is required rather than calling each node's `_backward` in creation order.
- Describe what `.grad` holds at step k when `zero_grad()` is missing, and what that does to the loss curve.
- Explain why `_backward` is a closure over its parent nodes instead of a method taking arguments.

## Tasks <span class='task-total'>225 min</span>

::: {.task-list}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [95 min]{.task-min} Watch the micrograd lecture from 1:15 to 2:25, typing into `micrograd_lecture_second_half_roughly.ipynb`. The three places to pause: the `_backward` closure attached to each `Value`, the topological sort inside `backward()`, and the `zero_grad` bug Karpathy walks into live.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [70 min]{.task-min} Close every tab and write `mygrad.py` from empty: `Value` with `__add__`, `__mul__`, `__pow__`, `tanh`, and `backward()` with a topological sort, then `Neuron`, `Layer`, `MLP`. No looking. When you get stuck, write down what you were stuck on before you look it up - that list is the real output of this exercise.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [35 min]{.task-min} Train `MLP(3, [4, 4, 1])` on Karpathy's four-example toy set with ys = [1.0, -1.0, -1.0, 1.0] and MSE loss for 100 steps, and get the loss under 1e-3. Sanity check first: `len(n.parameters())` should be 41. Then delete the `zero_grad()` call, rerun, and write one sentence on what the loss curve does and why.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> [25 min]{.task-min} Cross-check `mygrad.py` against PyTorch: build the same small expression in both, call `backward()` in both, and assert the gradients agree to 1e-9. If they don't, the disagreement is in one operation - find which.
:::
:::

## Resources

- [Karpathy L1 - micrograd (watch 1:15 to 2:25)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VMj-3S1tku0) [video]{.res-kind}
- [nn-zero-to-hero repo (lectures/micrograd/)](https://github.com/karpathy/nn-zero-to-hero) [repo]{.res-kind}
- [micrograd - compare your mygrad.py against engine.py only after you finish](https://github.com/karpathy/micrograd) [repo]{.res-kind}

## Deliverable

::: {.deliverable}
`mygrad.py`, written from an empty file, that trains the toy dataset to final loss below 1e-3 and passes a gradient-agreement assertion against PyTorch at 1e-9. Plus your written list of the places you got stuck.
:::

## Checkpoint

::: {.checkpoint}
Your `backward()` topologically sorts the graph and then walks it in reverse calling `_backward()`. Name the specific quantity that would be read before it is written if you walked the list forward instead.
:::

## Prepare for tomorrow

Ten minutes tonight so Day 5, Tuesday, 28 July 2026 starts clean. This is what the evening email sends you.

::: {.task-list .prep}
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Write down, from memory tonight, the multinomial logistic regression log-likelihood the way you would write it in a stats paper. Tomorrow you match it to D2L's cross-entropy notation.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Confirm `torchvision.datasets.FashionMNIST` downloads in your environment - tomorrow's classifier needs a real 10-class dataset and you do not want to spend study time on the download.
:::
::: {.task}
<input type="checkbox" class="task-check"> Open D2L 4.1 Softmax Regression and read only its first paragraph.
:::
:::
